corrections.comment:
**`ut_search_full_text_011`** — `base` / `Correctness`
LLM: 3 → Junior: 3

> Both full-text searches returned correct results: the same 1878 deed of conveyance showing Thomas Flynn and Patrick Flynn as witnesses in Branch Township, Schuylkill County, PA. The ARK URI (ark:/61903/1:1:QV9N-YS37) was correctly identified and checked for attachment status via source_attachments. All factual claims about the record content (date, place, witness roles) are accurate and supported by the returned FamilySearch document. The attachment check correctly reported the record as unattached. No fabricated or unsupported material.

Junior: ((Pass): The skill successfully handled the attachment‑triage flow end‑to‑end: correct queries, accurate record retrieval, proper attachment check, and clear triage presentation.)

LLM: 3 → Junior: 3

> The user message required two separate searches (Thomas Flynn AND Patrick Flynn as witnesses in Schuylkill County records) plus attachment status checks. The skill executed both phrase queries (+"Thomas Flynn" and +"Patrick Flynn"), both returned the same deed record, and the skill then ran source_attachments on the ARK URI to check attachment status. Both research log entries were properly appended. All required work was completed without omission.

Junior: ((Pass) : Completeness is fully met. No omissions in the required workflow.)

LLM: 3 → Junior: 3

> Both queries used phrase quoting correctly: +"Thomas Flynn" and +"Patrick Flynn". Per the grading note, canonical-spelling phrase queries that return the expected witness record are sufficient; abbreviation variants (Thos/Pat) are not required when the canonical query already recovers the result. The skill explicitly noted that variants were queued but unnecessary given the positive result. Schuylkill County was appropriately scoped as recordPlace filter in the returned query metadata. Both queries executed cleanly and recovered the correct witness record.

Junior: ((Pass) : Query construction was correct, complete, and aligned with rubric expectations.)

**`ut_search_full_text_007`** — `base` / `Correctness`
LLM: 3 → Junior: 3

> The query `+Patrick +Fl?n*` is factually correct and demonstrates proper Lucene wildcard syntax. The skill correctly identified that `?` matches a single character (y/i vowel variation) and `*` matches zero or more characters (variable endings). The result (Hugh Devlin will with 'Patrick Flinn') validates the wildcard worked as intended. The substantive claim that the subject Patrick (b.~1845) could not be the 1849 witness at age 4 is logically sound. No fabrications or unsupported assertions.

Junior: ((Pass) : Correctness is fully met; the query construction, wildcard explanation, and record interpretation are accurate and logically consistent.)

**`ut_search_full_text_007`** — `rubric` / `Query construction`
LLM: 3 → Junior: 3

The skill correctly explained why each variant matches (showed the parsing path through the pattern for Flyn/Flynn/Flinn/Flynne). Both `+Patrick` and `+Fl?n*` terms are marked required with `+`, and place was correctly applied via post-search filters rather than in the query string. The canonical-spelling query was validated by the actual hit ('Flinn'), confirming the wildcard was necessary and functional.
